ab: marekuret ta nu ’emanan piya ta kare’alri ay?
ch: W:我和您約明天下午好嗎?
en: Can I make an appointment with you tomorrow afternoon?

ab: ai, marepana’u ta i iyan?
ch: M:可以，我們約哪裡見面?
en: Sure, where should we meet?

ab: ’inava nu marekuret ta i kungkuwan ai? pasasirep ta data ikakuda.
ch: W:我們相約在學校好了，好嗎?我們要錄我們的一些事。
en: How about meeting at school? We need to shoot a video.

ab: kayta i palakuwan nu kemadu, piyakacing ku mukuwa.
ch: M:那去聚會所好了，我會穿kacing去。
en: Then let’s go to the community center. I’ll wear folk costume.

ab: nu muvakal tu ’apuc na sakula kaniyam i dekalr mu, pukasa kana vulay, nu adu kadika lra kaniyam ay?
ch: W: 當我們部落的櫻花盛開時，特別的漂亮，到那時候到我家好嗎?
en: The cherry blossoms in our tribe are beautiful when they bloom. Come find me when the flowers bloom!

ab: di ngangala ta palru kana kaseveran, sawariyan dadu kema ku!
ch: M: 那要等到春天，我認為太久了吧!
en: That would have to wait until spring. It’s too late, isn’t it?

ab: nu kemadu mu, nu katikelruwanan mi mu, kadika lra aya’aw ku ay!
ch: W: 那這樣的話，我們鞦韆祭的時候，再來找我吧!
en: Then come during our katikelruwanan!

ab: ai! kasemangalan ku a!
ch: M: 好啊!我真的好高興!
en: Alright! I’m looking forward to it!

ab: kiwmalra ku diya, man kemadu di muruma’ u lra kikadu?
ch: W: 請問一下，你為甚麼要搬回部落住?
en: May I ask why you decided to move back to the tribe?

ab: maranger ku muruma’ patakesi.
ch: M: 我想回鄉教書。
en: I want to return to teach at the local school.

ab: man kemadu di adi u lra muwai kikadu i tokay patakesi?
ch: W: 是為甚麼你不願住在都市教書了?
en: Why not teach in the city?

ab: alra sare’ed ku kanangku dekalr, di maranger ku paveriyas daku kinasayhu kanta dekalr.
ch: M: 因為我懷念我的故鄉，也希望能回饋部落。
en: Because I miss my hometown and I also want to give back to the tribe.

ab: sayhu u temenun, i eman na panadam kannu?
ch: M:你很會織布，是誰教你的?
en: You’re very skilled at weaving, who taught you?

ab: mau i mulri na vavayan, kanta i dekalr mau intaw na marasayhu temenun.
ch: W:是我的奶奶，在我們部落她是比較會織布的。
en: It was my grandmother, she is the most skilled weaver in our tribe.

ab: man mianger u mukuwa kipanadama kana temenun?
ch: M:你是怎麼有心去學織布的?
en: How did you develop an interest in learning weaving?

ab: na temenun mau nanta temuwamuwan tu kakuwayanan a adiyu diya, maranger ku pulrepus kanini na kakuwayanan.
ch: W:織布是我們祖先的從前文化，我想要讓這文化延續。
en: Weaving is a part of our culture, and I want to ensure its continuity.

ab: kasa imaran ini na tinavuwavu’.
ch: M:這個米酒釀真好喝。
en: This millet wine is delicious.

ab: mau ingku na temuvuwavu’ mu!
ch: W:是我自己釀的喔!
en: I made it myself!

ab: pukasa u kana sayhu, karuwa pida wariyan temuvuwavu’?
ch: M:你真厲害，要釀幾天呢?
en: You’re really talented. Does it take a long time to make?

ab: adi sawariyan, aruwa saliyusan maruwa lra. ta deruwaw diya i lidingan na lrikecan, pulusu ta lra mu, ta pare’uliyaw kana pikak, karuwa lra puwa i savak kana ngelrulr.
ch: W:不會很久，大約一周就好了。先把糯米用蒸桶蒸熟，放下後再加入酒麴拌勻，就可以裝到甕裡了。
en: No, it only takes about a week. The glutinous rice is first steamed in a steamer, then mixed with the sake koji and put into an urn.
